nodes.name: goals.ts
nodes.summary: Provides goal data access and a factory service for querying company goals from the database, including fetching the default active company-level goal.

nodes.name: getDefaultCompanyGoal
nodes.summary: Queries the database for the default active company-level goal for a given company, returning the first result ordered by creation date.

nodes.name: goalService
nodes.summary: Factory function that returns a service object with goal CRUD and query operations bound to the provided database instance.

nodes.name: heartbeat-run-summary.ts
nodes.summary: Provides utilities for summarizing and truncating heartbeat run result JSON, including merging run summaries and building issue comments from run results.

nodes.name: mergeHeartbeatRunResultJson
nodes.summary: Merges a heartbeat run result JSON object with a summary structure, normalizing and bounding fields for storage.

nodes.name: summarizeHeartbeatRunResultJson
nodes.summary: Extracts a structured summary from a raw heartbeat run result JSON, truncating fields to configured maximums for display and storage.

nodes.name: buildHeartbeatRunIssueComment
nodes.summary: Builds an issue comment object from a heartbeat run result JSON for posting run results as issue timeline events.

nodes.name: heartbeat-stop-metadata.ts
nodes.summary: Provides logic for inferring heartbeat run stop reasons, resolving timeout policies per adapter type, and building/merging stop metadata for run records.

nodes.name: resolveHeartbeatRunTimeoutPolicy
nodes.summary: Determines the effective timeout policy for a heartbeat run given the adapter type and adapter configuration, falling back to per-adapter defaults.

nodes.name: inferHeartbeatRunStopReason
nodes.summary: Infers the stop reason for a heartbeat run based on run state and timeout conditions, classifying outcomes as timeout, max-turn, or normal completion.

nodes.name: buildHeartbeatRunStopMetadata
nodes.summary: Constructs a stop metadata record for a heartbeat run by combining inferred stop reason with timing and policy details.

nodes.name: mergeHeartbeatRunStopMetadata
nodes.summary: Merges stop metadata into a heartbeat run result JSON object, persisting the stop reason and policy for downstream consumption.

nodes.name: heartbeat.ts
nodes.summary: The core heartbeat service managing AI agent execution runs: scheduling, executing, monitoring, logging, billing, workspace management, session state, and wake/continuation logic across the full lifecycle of agent runs.
nodes.languageNotes: At nearly 10K lines, this is the largest service in the codebase, containing 88 exported and internal functions and the primary heartbeatService factory function spanning lines 2313-9812.

nodes.name: heartbeatService
nodes.summary: The primary factory for the heartbeat service, providing all operations for managing agent run lifecycles including starting, stopping, monitoring, billing, and session continuation.

nodes.name: buildPaperclipWakePayload
nodes.summary: Constructs the full wake payload for a Paperclip agent run, assembling context snapshots, session overrides, workspace config, and interaction state for the next execution turn.

nodes.name: resolveExecutionRunAdapterConfig
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective adapter configuration for an execution run by merging issue settings, agent defaults, and run-scoped overrides.

nodes.name: enrichWakeContextSnapshot
nodes.summary: Enriches a wake context snapshot with additional runtime data such as interaction IDs and session metadata before passing it to the wake payload builder.

nodes.name: resolveRuntimeSessionParamsForWorkspace
nodes.summary: Determines the effective runtime session parameters (compaction policy, model profile, etc.) for an agent workspace before a run is dispatched.

nodes.name: buildPaperclipTaskMarkdown
nodes.summary: Renders a Paperclip task as structured Markdown for injection into agent context, including issue details, blockers, and continuation state.

nodes.name: hire-hook.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the notification hook triggered when an agent hire request is approved, sending relevant notifications to the requesting user.

nodes.name: notifyHireApproved
nodes.summary: Sends a notification to the appropriate user when an agent hire request is approved, creating the relevant activity or notification record.

nodes.name: inbox-dismissals.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a service factory for managing inbox dismissal records, allowing users to dismiss items from their issue inbox.

nodes.name: inboxDismissalService
nodes.summary: Factory function returning a service for creating and querying inbox dismissal records for a given database.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file that re-exports all service factories, utility functions, and type definitions from the services directory, providing a single unified import point for the entire services layer.

nodes.name: instance-settings.ts
nodes.summary: Manages instance-level configuration settings including general and experimental feature flags, with normalization and persistence logic for server instance settings.

nodes.name: instanceSettingsService
nodes.summary: Factory returning a service for reading and updating instance-level settings, with caching and validation of general and experimental configuration.

nodes.name: invite-grants.ts
nodes.summary: Provides utilities for deriving default permission grants for human and agent members joining a company via invites.

nodes.name: issue-approvals.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the issue approval service managing creation, querying, and lifecycle of approval requests attached to issues.

nodes.name: issueApprovalService
nodes.summary: Factory returning a service for managing issue approval records — creating, listing, and resolving approvals on issues.

nodes.name: issue-assignment-wakeup.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a utility to enqueue a wakeup request for an issue when it is assigned to an agent, ensuring the agent receives the assignment wake signal.

nodes.name: queueIssueAssignmentWakeup
nodes.summary: Inserts an agent wakeup request into the queue when an issue is assigned, triggering the assigned agent to start working.

nodes.name: issue-continuation-summary.ts
nodes.summary: Manages issue continuation summary documents that capture the agent's progress state — extracting next actions, mode inference, and refreshing the persisted summary document between runs.

nodes.name: buildContinuationSummaryMarkdown
nodes.summary: Builds a Markdown document summarizing the agent's continuation state, including mode, next action, and extracted work context from the run result.

nodes.name: getIssueContinuationSummaryDocument
nodes.summary: Retrieves the current continuation summary document for an issue from the database.

nodes.name: refreshIssueContinuationSummary
nodes.summary: Generates and upserts a fresh continuation summary document for an issue after a run completes, persisting the agent's progress state for future context.

nodes.name: issue-execution-policy.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the full state machine for issue execution policies including multi-stage approval workflows, monitor scheduling, and state transitions with side-effect patch generation.

nodes.name: applyIssueExecutionStageTransition
nodes.summary: Applies a stage transition in the multi-stage approval workflow, computing the resulting patch for issue state, assignees, and execution policy based on the actor's decision.

nodes.name: applyMonitorTransition
nodes.summary: Applies a monitor state transition for an issue, computing the patch to update monitor scheduling and wake state.

nodes.name: normalizeIssueExecutionPolicy
nodes.summary: Validates and normalizes an issue execution policy object, ensuring all stage configurations are well-formed and participants are valid.

nodes.name: applyIssueExecutionPolicyTransition
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point for applying an execution policy transition, dispatching to stage or monitor transition handlers as appropriate.

nodes.name: issue-goal-fallback.ts
nodes.summary: Provides fallback resolution logic for determining the appropriate goal ID for an issue when no explicit goal is set, traversing the issue tree and company defaults.

nodes.name: resolveNextIssueGoalId
nodes.summary: Resolves the next goal ID for an issue by walking up the parent hierarchy and falling back to the company default goal.

nodes.name: issue-liveness.ts
nodes.summary: Re-exports the issue graph liveness classifier and all related type definitions from the liveness analysis module.

nodes.name: issue-recovery-actions.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the service for managing issue recovery actions — creating, listing, and applying recovery entries that help agents self-heal from failure states.

nodes.name: issueRecoveryActionService
nodes.summary: Factory returning a service for CRUD operations on issue recovery actions, including conflict detection and bulk application to agent runs.

nodes.name: issue-references.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the issue reference service for querying and computing cross-issue relationships, including related work summaries and reference diff tracking.

nodes.name: issueReferenceService
nodes.summary: Factory returning a service for computing and persisting issue cross-references, including related work discovery and summary diffing.

nodes.name: issue-thread-interactions.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the issue thread interaction service handling agent-user interaction flows such as question/answer, task suggestions, confirmation requests, and their resolution lifecycle.

nodes.name: issueThreadInteractionService
nodes.summary: Factory returning a service for creating, resolving, accepting, rejecting, and managing all forms of agent-user interaction within issue threads.

nodes.name: hydrateInteraction
nodes.summary: Enriches a raw interaction database row with associated comments and documents to produce a fully hydrated interaction object.

nodes.name: resolveSelectedSuggestedTasks
nodes.summary: Resolves which suggested tasks have been selected by the user from a suggest-tasks interaction for creation as child issues.

nodes.name: issue-tree-control.ts
nodes.summary: Manages pause/hold and cancel operations on issue trees — applying tree-wide holds, restoring statuses, and verifying interaction wake requests for tree control actions.

nodes.name: issueTreeControlService
nodes.summary: Factory returning a service for applying and releasing pause/hold and cancel operations across issue trees, with verification and conflict detection.

nodes.name: isVerifiedIssueTreeControlInteractionWake
nodes.summary: Verifies that a wake request for issue tree control originated from a trusted interaction source before permitting the operation.

nodes.name: issues.ts
nodes.summary: The central issue service implementing the full CRUD lifecycle for issues including creation, status transitions, comment management, workspace configuration, blocker tracking, and productivity reviews.
nodes.languageNotes: At over 4K lines with 55 internal helper functions and the issueService factory spanning lines 1780–4470, this is one of the most critical services in the backend.

nodes.name: issueService
nodes.summary: The main issue service factory providing all issue lifecycle operations including creation, updates, comments, labels, attachments, assignments, and workspace config.

nodes.name: listIssueBlockerAttentionMap
nodes.summary: Computes a map of blocker attention entries for a set of issues, traversing dependency chains to determine which issues are actively blocked and by what.

nodes.name: deriveIssueUserContext
nodes.summary: Derives per-user contextual data for an issue such as read state, last comment timestamp, and participation flags for personalized issue views.

nodes.name: deriveIssueCommentRunLogAttribution
nodes.summary: Attributes issue comment run log entries to their originating heartbeat run, computing display metadata for agent-generated comments.

nodes.name: json-schema-secret-refs.ts
nodes.summary: Provides utilities for finding secret references within JSON schema config values, reading and writing config values at paths, and identifying UUID-based secret references.

nodes.name: live-events.ts
nodes.summary: Provides publish/subscribe utilities for broadcasting live events to company or global event streams, enabling real-time updates to connected clients.

nodes.name: publishLiveEvent
nodes.summary: Publishes a live event to the company-scoped event stream for real-time propagation to subscribed clients.

nodes.name: subscribeCompanyLiveEvents
nodes.summary: Returns a subscriber for the company-scoped live event stream, enabling downstream consumers to react to real-time events.

nodes.name: local-service-supervisor.ts
nodes.summary: Manages the lifecycle of local runtime services — tracking process registrations, detecting adoptable services, checking liveness, and terminating stale processes via a file-based registry.

nodes.name: terminateLocalService
nodes.summary: Sends termination signals to a local service process and waits for it to stop, cleaning up the registry record on success.

nodes.name: findAdoptableLocalService
nodes.summary: Searches the local service registry for a running process that can be adopted by a new run based on matching service key and liveness checks.

nodes.name: listLocalServiceRegistryRecords
nodes.summary: Reads and returns all records from the local service registry directory, normalizing each entry.

nodes.name: plugin-capability-validator.ts
nodes.summary: Validates plugin capability declarations against the set of allowed and required capabilities, ensuring plugins do not exceed their declared permissions or omit required entries.

nodes.name: pluginCapabilityValidator
nodes.summary: Factory returning a validator for plugin capability declarations, checking allowlists, required fields, and configuration schemas for each declared capability.

nodes.name: plugin-config-validator.ts
nodes.summary: Validates plugin instance configuration against the plugin's declared JSON schema, returning structured validation errors.

nodes.name: validateInstanceConfig
nodes.summary: Validates a plugin instance's config object against the plugin's JSON schema, returning a list of validation errors or null if valid.
